items.q: What is the function of the bridge in a pop song?
items.correct: To provide contrast before the final chorus, often by changing melody, key, or texture.
items.misconception: It is the same as the chorus.
items.random_wrong: It is the very first section.
items.why: Students confuse bridge with chorus because both are 'big' sections. The bridge appears once (usually after the second chorus) to provide CONTRAST and renew interest before returning.

items.q: What is characteristic of Baroque music?
items.correct: Continuous bass line, ornamentation, terraced dynamics, and counterpoint.
items.why: Students project later styles backwards. Baroque (1600-1750) used basso continuo, harpsichord/organ, and terraced (sudden) dynamics. Piano hadn't yet replaced harpsichord.

items.q: How does D Dorian differ from D minor?
items.correct: Dorian has a raised 6th (B natural) compared to natural minor.
items.random_wrong: Dorian has no sharps or flats at all.
items.why: Students treat modes as variants of major/minor. Dorian on D uses only white keys (like C major, but starting on D), giving a raised 6th compared to D natural minor - producing the modal flavour.

items.q: Which keyboard instrument is most associated with the Baroque era?
items.why: It's Harpsichord. “Piano” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which composer is NOT from the Classical period?
items.why: Correct answer: Chopin. “Haydn” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Approximately what year does the Baroque era end?
items.why: The real figure is 1750. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
